name: Design Tokens (DTCG ↔ CSS)
summary: Conformance reference for the design-token interchange + runtime mapping. The authoring/interchange format is W3C DTCG 2025.10 (adopted, not coined — the one legitimate Protocol here): a vendor-neutral JSON of { $type, $value } nodes with {group.token} aliasing, so tokens are portable across Style Dictionary, Tokens Studio, Figma, Terrazzo. The runtime tier is JS-first (ratified #1682, platform-decisions.md#tokens-js-first): the resolved token set's source of truth is JS, held in the injector (webinjectors/webcontexts), read synchronously and off-DOM by any consumer (constructor/pre-attach compute, worker/OffscreenCanvas, console %c, SSR, tests — none of which can read a CSS custom property). From that one source a CSS custom property (--<group>-<token>) is emitted ONE-WAY (JS→CSS) for the declarative paint path — never hand-authored in parallel, never the source; typed numerics register via @property, schemes resolve through light-dark()/color-scheme, contrast through prefers-contrast, and derived accent/state scales through relative color syntax (oklch(from …)) + color-mix() — validated against WCAG/APCA per step (the contrast-validation gate; curated scales are a brand-precision override, not the accessibility net). The standard ships a COMPLETE default token set; a consuming project overrides via DTCG `extends` rather than authoring from scratch. The taxonomy is 3-tier (primitive · semantic · component); the SEMANTIC tier is NOT defined here — it is owned by the Web Intents (density, surface, typography, motion, theme-color), whose refs resolve INTO a primitive token. NOT this protocol: the semantic role vocabulary (Web Intents) and the theme-color intent authoring (#010).
